source_description: Inside an apartment, Monica and Chandler sit on a sofa, while Joey sits on an armchair and Phoebe sits on a stool near the coffee table. Background music is audible. Rachel enters through the front door, followed by Ross.

Inside an apartment, Monica and Chandler sit on a sofa, while Joey sits on an armchair and Phoebe sits on a stool near the coffee table. Background music is audible. Rachel enters through the front door, followed by Ross.

Inside an apartment, Monica and Chandler sit on a sofa, while Joey sits on an armchair and Phoebe sits on a stool near the coffee table. Background music is audible. Rachel enters through the front door, followed by Ross.

Rachel walks into the kitchen area, turns back toward Ross, and says, "Cannot believe that you didn't tell me we are still married," her volume high and pace fast. Ross stands near the dining table, hands held out with palms up, and replies, "Look, I was going to tell you." Rachel places her hands on her hips and asks, "When? After the birth of our first secret child?"

Rachel turns toward the group in the living room and says, "Ross didn't get the annulment? We are still married." Monica's eyebrows rise and her mouth parts as she asks, "What?" Phoebe says, "Oh, my God," while looking toward Ross. Audience laughter is audible.

Rachel turns toward the group in the living room and says, "Ross didn't get the annulment? We are still married." Monica's eyebrows rise and her mouth parts as she asks, "What?" Phoebe says, "Oh, my God," while looking toward Ross. Audience laughter is audible.

Rachel turns toward the group in the living room and says, "Ross didn't get the annulment? We are still married." Monica's eyebrows rise and her mouth parts as she asks, "What?" Phoebe says, "Oh, my God," while looking toward Ross. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica maintains her gaze on Ross, her mouth still slightly open. Ross stands still behind the kitchen table, his hands lowered to his sides.
Monica maintains her gaze on Ross, her mouth still slightly open. Ross stands still behind the kitchen table, his hands lowered to his sides.
Monica says, "Ross." Ross steps forward slightly and says, "Okay, maybe it wasn't my best decision but I just couldn't face another failed marriage," his hands gesturing outward.
Monica says, "Ross." Ross steps forward slightly and says, "Okay, maybe it wasn't my best decision but I just couldn't face another failed marriage," his hands gesturing outward.
Monica says, "Ross." Ross steps forward slightly and says, "Okay, maybe it wasn't my best decision but I just couldn't face another failed marriage," his hands gesturing outward.
Chandler leans forward toward Ross and says, "Okay, let me just jump in and ask."
Chandler leans forward toward Ross and says, "Okay, let me just jump in and ask."
Chandler continues, "At what point did you think this was a successful marriage?" his pace fast. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stands with her arms crossed over her chest, her gaze fixed on Ross. Ross looks down at the table and then back up at Rachel.
Ross steps closer to Rachel and says, "Rach, come on. If you think about it, it's actually kind of funny." His volume rises slightly on "funny." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross steps closer to Rachel and says, "Rach, come on. If you think about it, it's actually kind of funny." His volume rises slightly on "funny." Audience laughter is audible.

Rachel continues to look at Ross with her arms crossed. Ross maintains his position, facing her.
Ross looks toward the floor and says, "Maybe it's best not to think about it," his volume lower than his previous line. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks toward the floor and says, "Maybe it's best not to think about it," his volume lower than his previous line. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel remains still, her head turned toward Ross.
Phoebe sits on a stool, looking away from Ross, and says, "This is inexcusable. I'm shocked to my very core." She places both hands on her chest. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe sits on a stool, looking away from Ross, and says, "This is inexcusable. I'm shocked to my very core." She places both hands on her chest. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe sits on a stool, looking away from Ross, and says, "This is inexcusable. I'm shocked to my very core." She places both hands on her chest. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel and Ross remain in the kitchen area. Ross looks toward Phoebe while Rachel continues to face him.
Ross points a finger toward Phoebe and says, "Phoebe, I told her you already knew."
Monica, Chandler, and Joey turn their heads to look at Phoebe. Phoebe looks toward the group, then shifts her gaze back toward Ross.
Monica, Chandler, and Joey turn their heads to look at Phoebe. Phoebe looks toward the group, then shifts her gaze back toward Ross.
Monica, Chandler, and Joey turn their heads to look at Phoebe. Phoebe looks toward the group, then shifts her gaze back toward Ross.
Phoebe points a finger at Ross and says, "Another lie. You have a sickness," her pace fast. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross stands still with a brief smile on his face, looking toward Phoebe. Rachel remains in the background with her arms crossed.
Ross stands still with a brief smile on his face, looking toward Phoebe. Rachel remains in the background with her arms crossed.
Chandler says, "Ross, just for my own peace of mind you're not married to any more of us, are you?" his volume rising at the end of the sentence. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "Ross, just for my own peace of mind you're not married to any more of us, are you?" his volume rising at the end of the sentence. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stands with her hands on her hips, looking at Ross. Ross keeps his gaze on Chandler.
Ross turns to face Rachel and says, "Look, Rach, I was going to tell you. I just wanted to do it, um, you know, my own way." He moves his hands in small circles in front of his chest.
Ross turns to face Rachel and says, "Look, Rach, I was going to tell you. I just wanted to do it, um, you know, my own way." He moves his hands in small circles in front of his chest.
Ross turns to face Rachel and says, "Look, Rach, I was going to tell you. I just wanted to do it, um, you know, my own way." He moves his hands in small circles in front of his chest.
Rachel continues to look at Ross, her arms now crossed.
Rachel says, "Ah, yes, silently, with no words," her pace fast and volume high. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel walks past Ross and exits the kitchen. Joey stands up from his chair and walks toward the kitchen area.
Rachel walks past Ross and exits the kitchen. Joey stands up from his chair and walks toward the kitchen area.
Joey says, "Hey," while raising one hand.
Joey stops walking and stands between Ross and the area where Rachel was standing.
Joey says, "Look, in Ross's defense, okay," his volume sustained.
Joey says, "Look, in Ross's defense, okay," his volume sustained.
Joey stands with his left hand raised and his mouth slightly open, looking between Ross and the doorway. Ross and the others watch him.

Joey turns and walks toward the refrigerator, opening the door. Audience laughter is audible.

Inside the coffee shop Central Perk, a blond man behind the counter writes on a notepad. Monica and Joey stand at the counter nearby. Text on screen reads "EXECUTIVE PRODUCER ADAM CHASE". Joey says, "You it. No."

Joey picks up a set of keys from the counter and says, "Hey, someone left their keys. OOH, to a Porsche." Monica looks at the keys. Text on screen reads "EXECUTIVE PRODUCER GREG MALINS". Audience laughter is audible.

Joey raises the keys toward the blond man behind the counter and asks, "Hey, Gunther. Are these yours?" The man looks at Joey and says, "Yeah, that's what I drive."
Joey raises the keys toward the blond man behind the counter and asks, "Hey, Gunther. Are these yours?" The man looks at Joey and says, "Yeah, that's what I drive."
The man behind the counter looks at Joey, his expression still. Joey continues to hold the keys.

Joey pulls out a brown leather shoe and says, "My shoe." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey holds the shoe up and looks at it. Monica stands behind him. Text on screen reads "SUPERVISING PRODUCER SHANA GOLDBERG-MEEHAN".

Joey picks up a small notepad and pen from the counter and says, "I'm going to go find that guy's car and leave a note on the windshield." He begins walking away from the counter. Text on screen reads "SUPERVISING PRODUCER SCOTT SILVERI".

Chandler looks up from his magazine and says, "Oh, good. And when he comes back for his keys, I'll be sure to give him your shoe," his pace fast. Text on screen reads "SUPERVISING PRODUCER ANDREW REICH". Audience laughter is audible.

Joey stops walking, turns back toward Chandler, and says, "Great."
Inside a coffee shop, Monica and Chandler sit on an orange couch. Phoebe walks toward them, holding a red jacket over her left arm.
Inside a coffee shop, Monica and Chandler sit on an orange couch. Phoebe walks toward them, holding a red jacket over her left arm.
Phoebe stands behind the couch and says, "Good, you guys are here. Listen," while adjusting her blonde hair with her right hand. Monica and Chandler look up at her.
Phoebe continues, "how would you like to spend tomorrow taking care of three incredibly cute little puppies?" her volume rising on "puppies." Her hands rest on her hips.
Phoebe continues, "how would you like to spend tomorrow taking care of three incredibly cute little puppies?" her volume rising on "puppies." Her hands rest on her hips.

Chandler looks toward Phoebe and says, "Yeah." Audience laughter is audible.
